name: Possible Ransomware Deletion of Volume Shadow Copies
description: Detects commands commonly used by ransomware to delete Volume Shadow Copies (VSS). Ransomware often deletes VSS to prevent victims from easily recovering their files. This rule identifies the use of `vssadmin` and `wmic` with specific parameters to delete shadow copies. It also includes an `excluded_users` list.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that executed the command (e.g., 'vssadmin.exe', 'wmic.exe').

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used, including arguments for deleting shadow copies.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the command was executed, excluding SYSTEM accounts.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
